style_notes: Southern and island-influenced names with bou, vel, wis, and srel stems.
Soft vowel bridges lead into clipped -el, -em, -un, and -ves endings.
Old Azhoran civic names with liquid consonants and open vowels.
Often favors cael, vel, and azh roots for rivers, coasts, and sacred lineages.
Highland and volcanic names with py, vor, vel, and neth stems.
Compounds tend toward strong initial consonants and -oss, -ell, or -marr endings.
Desert and oasis names with long vowels, hissing consonants, and compact endings.
Common roots distinguish wells, stone ridges, caravans, and old sacred sites.
Woodland and grassland names with Germanic-like th, v, and l clusters.
Favors compact roots for homesteads, woods, border stones, and old clearings.
Forest Mittoli endonym built from Ibenwood roots and older dense substrate forms.
Favors ib, ibn, thren, and glom clusters for old forest, silence, and unseen-presence concepts.
Elodi names preserve an eastern Old Kingdom lineage rather than an Azhoran family.
Koleth and Balog roots mark scriptural, administrative, and sacred authority registers.
